name: cache_stale

confirmed_fact_templates.text: Trace data confirms the request or entity entered an observable service flow.
A related persisted record exists in the database.
A related cache or idempotency key exists.
The related cache key still has a positive TTL.
The cached state and persisted state do not match.
The related cache key is absent.

default_confirmed_facts: The incident is being evaluated through the cache-versus-persistence runbook.

rules.root_cause: The cache value conflicts with the persisted state and is the most likely reason for the incorrect view.
alternative_hypotheses: The API may be reading from an outdated replica or alternate cache namespace.
recommended_next_actions: Inspect cache invalidation or refresh logic for the entity.
Check whether the cache write path lags behind the database commit.
If safe, invalidate the stale key and verify the API view again.

fallback.root_cause: The available evidence is insufficient to isolate a single root cause.
alternative_hypotheses: The missing signal may exist in MQ or log data that is not part of the MVP fixture set.
recommended_next_actions: Collect one more discriminating signal such as MQ or log evidence.
Narrow the incident window and retry the investigation.
Add a more specialized runbook for this symptom pattern.
